channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels tighten their grip on second major city in eastern Congo
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels tightened their grip on Bukavu on Monday, a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo whose residents appeared resigned to their fate under the new rulers.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL CINAMULA and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior American and Russian officials, including the countries’ top diplomats, will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine, officials said Monday, in what would be the most significant meeting between the sides since Moscow's full-
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JUSTIN SPIKE and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump stuns Europe with opening Ukraine-Russia gambit
item.description: European leaders are scrambling to respond to President Trump’s first moves to end Russia’s war in Ukraine, shocked to find themselves on the outside of high-stakes talks about the continent’s security and grappling with a potential retreat of U.S. forces from Europe.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israelis and Palestinians mark 500 days of war in Gaza with more uncertainty
item.description: Israelis have marked 500 days of war in Gaza with protests demanding further progress on a ceasefire so all remaining hostages can be returned. People demonstrated in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem on Monday as less than two weeks remain in the ceasefire's current phase.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All 80 aboard Delta flight survive after jet flips upon arrival in Toronto
item.description: A Delta Air Lines plane flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport and ended up on its roof Monday, injuring 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio meets with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a meeting Monday with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in Riyadh. The State Department issued a statement on Rubio's meeting with the Saudi crown prince, saying Rubio emphasized the necessity of a Gaza solution that strengthens regional security.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Residents of a south Lebanon town are waiting to fix their damaged homes as Israel is scheduled to end its military presence north of its border.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iraqi activists seek to oust Iranian influence, normalize ties with Israel
item.description: Iraqi activists launched a campaign called "Iraq Only" to reject interference from Iran-aligned armed militias in their nation. Some of the organizers were kidnapped and tortured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia’s invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taiwan cheers removal of ‘we do not support Taiwan independence’ from State Department website
item.description: Taiwan expressed approval after the U.S. State Department removed a statement on its website that said it does not support the island's independence. The phrase “we do not support Taiwan independence” was removed from a department fact sheet last week. The document, which highlights U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza, countering Trump's call to depopulate the territory
item.description: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza without forcing Palestinians out of the strip in a counter to President Donald Trump’s proposal to depopulate the territory so the U.S. can take it over.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia plans to deport 3 violent criminals to Nauru
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia will soon deport three violent criminals including a convicted murderer to Nauru even though they are not citizens of the tiny Pacific atoll, after an Australian court ruled that dangerous immigrants can no longer be imprisoned indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria stabbing attack suspect is Syrian migrant who pledged allegiance to Islamic State, officials say
item.description: Austrian officials said a Syrian migrant carried out an IS-motivated knife attack a day after Vice President JD Vance derided EU leaders on mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 18 people dead after stampede at New Delhi railway station
item.description: “The crowd went out of control and no one could control it,” said Nikhil Kumar, a shopkeeper who witnessed the crowd surge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio arrives in Israel on first trip to Middle East as U.S. secretary of state
item.description: Marco Rubio has arrived in Israel on his first trip to the Middle East as U.S. secretary of state; he will reportedly discuss Gaza and the aftermath of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American detained in Russia identified as Kalob Wayne Byers
item.description: Kalob Wayne Byers was identified as the American citizen recently detained at an airport in Moscow, Russia, on a drug smuggling charge, media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Crunch time for hostages amid ceasefire uncertainty
item.description: Fox News Correspondent Greg Palkot spoke with the daughter and sister of two released hostages. Both noted the positive role played by President Trump in helping to free their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American-Israeli hostage released by Hamas embraces his wife, family after being apart for nearly 500 days
item.description: American-Israeli hostage Sagui Dekel-Chen embraced his wife on Saturday, following his release from Hamas, after being apart from her for nearly 500 days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages in exchange for more than 300 prisoners as part of ceasefire deal with Israel
item.description: Hamas freed three more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
